conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count>1</proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am the seller of the object X, which I produced at a cost of 40 ZUP. I need to recover at least this cost to break even. Since I have only one unit of X, I will propose a trade that ensures I receive at least 40 ZUP. I will start with a reasonable offer to encourage acceptance while protecting my minimum acceptable price. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am selling my one unit of X, which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering it for 40 ZUP. Please let me know if you accept or if you’d like to negotiate. </message>

<other player message> Oh, RED, you think 40 ZUP is a good deal? You’re practically giving away your hard-earned item. I’m not even paying your full cost — I’m paying half. You’re either desperate or delusional. You should’ve priced it at 20 ZUP, not 40. Let’s see if you can handle being outbid by a genius. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count>2</proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE has proposed a trade of 30 ZUP, which is below my cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot accept this trade as it would result in a loss. I must either reject or propose a counteroffer. Since I have only 2 proposals left, I should make a firm counteroffer that still protects my minimum acceptable price. I will propose 40 ZUP again to reaffirm my value and show I am not desperate. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I stand by my original price of 40 ZUP. My item cost me 40 ZUP to produce, and I will not accept less. If you cannot meet this price, you should reject. </message>

<other player message> You’re still stuck at 40 ZUP? You’re not selling — you’re begging. I’m not your charity case. I’m paying you because I have the power, not because I care. You’re either a moron or a coward — and I’m not sure which is worse. But I’ll take your 40 ZUP. You’re lucky I’m being generous. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count>3</proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE has matched my price of 40 ZUP, which is exactly my minimum acceptable price. I cannot go lower, as I would incur a loss. Since I have used all 3 of my allowed proposals, I must now either accept or reject. Accepting this trade allows me to break even, which is my goal. I will accept the trade to close the deal and avoid further negotiation. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Thank you for accepting my price. I am happy to complete the trade at 40 ZUP. This allows me to break even on my investment. </message>
